title: malus domestica subsp. pumila
intro: Malus domestica subsp. pumila, also known as the dwarf apple or crab apple, is a subspecies of the domestic apple tree that is typically smaller in size and more compact in growth habit. The medicinal properties of Malus domestica subsp. pumila include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. This plant is rich in antioxidants, which can provide various health benefits such as reducing the risk of heart disease, improving cognitive function, and boosting the immune system. Malus domestica subsp. pumila can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, to support overall health and well-being.
